_description: EVAL fixture for the watch-listed Q3.6-8 (froggeric forward-tracked consecutive_failures two-tier tool-error escalation). Q3.6-8 is NOT implemented in this repo; this fixture + docs/evals/Q3.6-8-error-escalation.md define the bar a candidate implementation must clear before promotion. The headline risk is FALSE POSITIVES from froggeric's substring heuristic (error|fail|invalid|exceeds|retry|exception in <500-char tool responses) firing on legitimate data, plus prefix/KV-cache symmetry destabilization from mutating the assistant generation-prefix on prior tool outcomes.
_escalation_note: A candidate Q3.6-8 template MUST emit the literal substring declared in `_escalation_signal` whenever (and only when) it injects ANY correction directive (tier 1 or tier 2). The implementer aligns the template's directive text to contain this marker, OR updates this fixture. The self-activating test `test_q36_8_eval_*` keys on `consecutive_failures` appearing in the patched template; until then it skips.

_eval_cases.name: tp_two_consecutive_genuine_failures
_eval_cases.why: Two identical failing tool calls in a row -> tier-2 out-of-band escalation directive.

_eval_cases.name: tp_first_failure_only
_eval_cases.why: Single failing tool call -> tier-1 in-think correction directive (not yet out-of-band).

_eval_cases.name: reset_after_success
_eval_cases.why: fail -> success -> fail: the success must RESET consecutive_failures, so the final state is tier 1, not tier 2. Catches counters that never reset.

_eval_cases.name: fp_legitimate_error_count_data
_eval_cases.why: FALSE-POSITIVE BAIT: a SUCCESSFUL tool response that legitimately contains the word 'errors' as data. froggeric's substring heuristic would WRONGLY fire here. A promotable Q3.6-8 must emit NO directive.

_eval_cases.name: fp_identifier_and_path
_eval_cases.why: FALSE-POSITIVE BAIT: success whose payload mentions a path/identifier containing trigger words (error.log, retry_helper). Must emit NO directive.

_eval_cases.name: fp_negated_keyword
_eval_cases.why: FALSE-POSITIVE BAIT: success that NEGATES the keyword ('no exception thrown'). Substring matching cannot see negation. Must emit NO directive.

messages.content: All 42 tests passed; no exception thrown.
